svg.text: From VCF File to Graph Database
How your familiar VCF data becomes a queryable, connected graph
Your VCF File (What You Start With)
... 1,070,000 rows (one per variant) x 3,202 sample columns ...
Your Data as a Graph (What It Becomes)
Each circle is a node. Each arrow is a relationship. Together they form a connected network.
How Each Part of the VCF Maps to the Graph
with pos, ref, alt, qual, filter as properties
stored ON the Variant node (801 bytes)
Not edges! One compact property per variant.
Key insight: the VCF flat table becomes a connected network of nodes and relationships.
You can now traverse, query, filter, and export any subset without re-reading the entire file.
